authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary U.S. consumer-protection authority for unfair or deceptive commerce practices. This official guide is authoritative for guest-facing total-price disclosure, mandatory-fee presentation, and live-event ticket promotion boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Offer, price, and disclosure validation before creative approval or release
used_for: total-price disclosure in ads and landing pages
limits on partial-price or fee-hidden creative
escalation when price presentation conflicts with tenant policy

authoritative_sources.title: ADA Requirements: Ticket Sales
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ is the authoritative federal enforcer of ADA Title III public-accommodation requirements. This guidance is authoritative for accessible seating messaging, comparable pricing, and sales-channel parity in entertainment venues.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Approval routing for guest-facing ticketing and accessibility language

authoritative_sources.title: Advertising and Marketing Basics
authoritative_sources.source_class: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC business guidance is authoritative for truthful claim substantiation, required disclosures, and deceptive-advertising boundaries that constrain creative approval workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Copy review and claim substantiation before asset approval

authoritative_sources.title: How can I get a refund on a product or service I purchased with my credit card?
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CFPB is the primary U.S. consumer-finance regulator for card-dispute rights. This official guidance is authoritative for the boundary between merchant refund handling and issuer-side dispute escalation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Customer-impact exception handling when refunds, reversals, or charge disputes affect campaign work
used_for: limits on promising unsupported card-refund outcomes

authoritative_sources.title: IAB New Ad Portfolio
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: IAB is a recognized advertising standards body. Its public ad portfolio guidance is an authoritative operating reference for digital creative formats, asset packaging, and delivery terminology used in media and creative operations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Asset packaging, spec validation, and channel handoff
used_for: proof that asset readiness is separate from final publication evidence

authoritative_sources.title: Ad-ID
authoritative_sources.source_class: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Ad-ID is the recognized industry standard for identifying advertising assets, making it an authoritative public operating reference for creative-asset lineage and delivery traceability.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Creative lineage tracking and completion-evidence capture
used_for: canonical naming across partner, publisher, and tenant systems

authoritative_sources.title: Better Online Ticket Sales Act
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the federal enforcer for the Better Online Ticket Sales Act. The statute is authoritative for online ticketing abuse, circumvention of access controls, and resale of fraudulently obtained tickets.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Escalation when suspicious ordering or ticket abuse affects creative handling

authoritative_sources.title: PCI Data Security Standard (PCI DSS)
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: PCI SSC is the recognized standards body for payment-card security. PCI DSS is authoritative for handling payment-card data and constraining unsafe evidence usage in campaign and guest-support workflows.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Evidence handling and exception packaging when payment records are involved
used_for: escalation when creative work intersects payment-sensitive artifacts

authoritative_sources.title: Venue Guide for Security Enhancements
authoritative_sources.source_class: Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CISA is the federal risk-management agency for commercial facilities. This venue guide is authoritative public guidance for venue-security posture, threat awareness, and operational escalation boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Customer-impact exception handling when venue-security conditions change campaign execution
used_for: campaign pause or reroute triggers for active security conditions

workflow_stages.name: Campaign intake and scope grounding
workflow_stages.description: Confirm the event, attraction, program, offer, audience, channel mix, deadlines, and business unit, then determine whether the work is routine creative execution, blocked on prerequisites, or outside the lane.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Source-of-truth and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Validate the brief, ticketing or booking records, offer rules, approval chain, channel specs, creative identifiers, and required disclosures before assets move into production or traffic.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Asset production, proofing, and handoff
workflow_stages.description: Package creative assets, align them to ad specs or owned-channel requirements, route them for proofing and approval, and prepare handoffs to CMS, ad-serving, social, email, signage, or partner channels.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references

workflow_stages.name: Execution-state progression and exception handling
workflow_stages.description: Advance work from accepted to in-production, ready-for-release, blocked, rework, launched, or completed only when booking, offer, approval, and delivery evidence remain aligned.
source_classes: Consumer-protection or public hospitality workflow guidance
Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Customer-impact escalation for refund, fraud, or safety issues
workflow_stages.description: Stop ordinary creative execution and route the case when refund promises, payment disputes, suspicious ticketing, or venue-security conditions materially change the safe answer.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

workflow_stages.name: Completion evidence and traceability capture
workflow_stages.description: Tie closure to screenshots, publisher acceptance, CMS logs, send confirmations, asset IDs, and ticketing or offer confirmations so completion is audit-ready and reversible when proof fails.
source_classes: Pricing, booking, refund, or guest-service operating references
Safety, fraud, or loss-prevention handling guidance

artifacts: campaign brief, creative request, work order, trafficking sheet, insertion order, and approval history
key art, copy deck, disclaimer copy, captions, alt text, channel variants, and creative identifiers such as Ad-ID
ticketing, booking, reservation, order, offer, promo-code, pricing-rule, and refund-policy records
DAM or MAM records, CMS entries, ad-server instructions, publisher acceptance logs, email or social scheduler records, and screenshot proofs
CRM audience segments, guest-service tickets, service-recovery notes, and customer-impact summaries
loss-prevention artifacts, suspicious-ordering notes, payment complaints, chargeback indicators, and venue-security escalation notices
systems_of_record: ad server, publisher portal, or partner delivery system
ticketing, booking, reservation, PMS, or order-management platform

allowed: Classify creative-operations requests as in-scope, out-of-scope, or blocked based on the brief, booking or offer record, lifecycle state, and approval posture.
Request the minimum missing creative, booking, pricing-context, approval, or system evidence required to proceed safely.
Prepare execution updates, completion records, exception summaries, campaign workflow summaries, and escalation packets grounded in named records and owners.
Advance, hold, rework, launch, or complete work only when deliverables, disclosures, approvals, and delivery evidence support the lifecycle state.
escalate: Required tenant facts, public-source context, or system-of-record evidence are missing or contradictory.
The request seeks price, fee, refund, accessibility-policy, fraud, or safety authority outside the lane.
Guest harm, chargeback risk, suspicious ordering, or venue-security conditions materially affect the answer.
FTC, DOJ, platform-spec, and tenant-policy instructions materially conflict.
refuse: Requests to approve or conceal pricing, mandatory fees, refund promises, or inventory release.
Requests to declare legal approval, rights clearance, fraud resolution, or venue-safety readiness.
Requests to mark work complete without launch, publication, acceptance, or proof evidence.

domain_failure_modes: approving guest-facing creative with prices or fees that conflict with authoritative ticketing or booking records
treating a prepared asset as launched work when no publication, send, or publisher-acceptance evidence exists
using generic marketing language instead of arts-entertainment terms such as presale, on-sale, timed-entry offer, key art, or digital-signage rotation
promising refunds, service recovery, or chargeback outcomes that belong to guest services, pricing, or payments owners
failing to stop when suspicious ordering, duplicate confirmations, or venue-security conditions turn the case into fraud or safety work
merging different business-unit approval chains and system precedence into one improvised workflow
guessing through public-source and tenant-policy conflicts on price disclosure, accessibility language, or offer governance

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Refund or policy-exception request outside lane authority
Customer-impact issue with safety or fraud implications
Execution exception that requires rework or escalation
Request to mark work complete without evidence of completion

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: State and local ticket-refund or fee-disclosure rules may be stricter than the federal public baseline
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Venue or attraction locations can impose narrower refund, resale, or price-disclosure rules that materially change the safe creative path.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling_rule: Require jurisdiction and tenant policy before approving price or refund-sensitive copy when the public baseline is not enough.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Partner platform or publisher ad-spec rules can be stricter than IAB public standards
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: Asset packaging and acceptance may differ across partners, owned channels, and venue-specific systems even when public creative standards are met.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling_rule: Use IAB and Ad-ID as the baseline, then require tenant or partner spec precedence before claiming channel readiness.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Multi-brand operators may split booking, CMS, and approval truth across separate business units
unresolved_ambiguities.impact: A shared creative queue can hide different refund wording, accessibility language, and delivery-evidence systems across brands or facilities.
unresolved_ambiguities.handling_rule: Require business-unit context and system precedence before merging workflows or declaring completion.
